commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the Codex app-server terminal-idle timeout resolver and call site so the default watchdog follows the effective run timeout, with focused resolver tests.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +16. Total +29 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection rather than by running a 30-minute scheduled turn: current main and v2026.5.27 resolve the terminal-idle default to 1800000ms without using params.timeoutMs, and the PR proof shows the real resolver returning 2700000ms for a 45-minute budget.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair lane is needed; the branch is focused and review-clean, with remaining action limited to normal maintainer review and check gating.

target.title: fix(codex): derive terminal-idle watchdog from effective run timeout
target.body:
## Summary
Refs #85242. Derives the Codex app-server terminal-idle watchdog from the effective run timeout instead of a hardcoded 30-minute default.

This addresses the early-abort half of #85242 as a focused runtime fix. The diagnostic-wording half (naming the terminal-idle watchdog and the effective timeout in the surfaced guidance) is left as a separate follow-up, per the maintainer scope note. So this PR uses `Refs` rather than `Fixes`.

Rebased onto current main: the resolver moved to `attempt-timeouts.ts` in the upstream app-server split, so the derivation now lives there (reusing the existing `resolvePositiveIntegerTimeoutMs` helper, matching the neighbouring post-tool resolver) and the call site in `run-attempt.ts` passes `params.timeoutMs`.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: the terminal-idle watchdog used a hardcoded 30-minute default (CODEX_TURN_TERMINAL_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS) not derived from the effective run timeout, so a scheduled turn configured with a longer timeoutSeconds was aborted early at 30 minutes. The resolver now follows the run budget.

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js 24.14.0, current PR head rebased on main. Invoked the real resolveCodexTurnTerminalIdleTimeoutMs from attempt-timeouts.ts directly under node.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the real resolver against the runtime via node.

Evidence after fix: stdout from `node --import tsx` invoking the real resolver:
node --import tsx proof85242.mjs
resolve(undefined)             ->  1800000 ms   (no run budget; keeps the 30 minute default; backward compatible)
resolve(undefined, 45min)      ->  2700000 ms   (45 minute budget; follows budget; not cut to 30 minutes)
resolve(undefined, 10min)      ->  1800000 ms   (10 minute budget; 30 minute floor held)
resolve(5min, 45min)           ->   300000 ms   (explicit override wins; ignores budget)
resolve(undefined, Infinity)   ->  1800000 ms   (non-finite budget; keeps default)

Observed result after fix: a run with a 45-minute effective timeout now resolves a 45-minute terminal-idle ceiling (2700000 ms) rather than the prior 1800000 ms ceiling, so the watchdog no longer aborts the run early. With no run budget the resolver still returns the 30-minute default, preserving backward compatibility. A budget below the floor keeps the 30-minute floor, so existing protection is never shortened. The shorter completion-idle and post-tool idle watchdogs are unchanged.

What was not tested: a live 30-minute scheduled Codex turn was not run end to end; the derivation is exercised through the real resolver invoked under node plus focused unit tests in attempt-timeouts.test.ts. The diagnostic-wording half of #85242 is intentionally out of scope here and left as a follow-up.
